module_mood_contract: grounded competence — 'I can feel when a plan is good before approving it' (M2 opener primer; resolves into the exercise, not in the lecture)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; vocabulary lectures land mid-module; demo lectures open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture sits at M2 open per strategy doc; teaches vocabulary (three things / three pressures) that the exercise will earn through living it. The retro label 'plan-mode approval inflation' is correctly NOT named (maintainer block line 64 confirms it's held for exercise P5).
M2 is not a contrast-mood module (that is M5). Strategy doc M2 = grounded competence, not learn-through-doing-name-after.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honored; teaching-beat mood floor 8+/10.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Mood = grounded competence (line 65 maintainer); trace phase 1 mood_score=8, close=8. Session widget (lines 3-9) classified as SETUP beat (mechanical staging, on rails), mood-exempt per teaching-vs-setup carve-out — judged on clarity + brevity, clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 15 leads with 'Plan mode in Claude Code is a permission state, not a feature' — positive discipline (permission state, two reads paired). Three pressures (lines 42-44) name the trap, but the lecture's frame is the discipline.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — don't front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M2 detail (line 302): 'plan-mode approval inflation' label lands retroactively at exercise P5. Lecture honors this — maintainer block line 64 confirms the label is withheld from body. Close (line 48) telegraphs the exercise shape without naming the retro pattern.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts (theme instances per module).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture file — Key Concepts section lives in the M2 module file, not the lecture. Lecture body honors theme #4 (90% correct / 'agreement is cheap; the read is what matters') and theme #5 (mechanically self-aware via the optional ask-plan-mode-directly beat).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture is a primer; forcing functions (push-back x2, walk-down branches, approve-then-stop) live in the exercise file, not here. Body prose names what will happen in the exercise, doesn't enact discipline. Optional ask-plan-mode-directly beat carries its own action lead-in (line 25) before the fenced prompt at line 27.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; the trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversations, no 'share with your neighbor', no pause cues, no comparison prompts. Body is about the work (what plan mode is, three things, three pressures, the exercise shape).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Session widget source shape; place by learning flow.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 3-9 use the canonical source shape with rename slug inside fenced block (CLI-only auto-wrap). Placement is lecture-top — interactive lecture (carries optional {{prompt:when-a-plan-is-good-1}} at line 27), so pattern (c) applies. Module-opener context: this is the M2 lecture sitting first in the module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body around {{prompt:when-a-plan-is-good-1}}: lines 22-25 name the activity ('Enter plan mode... ask Claude what shifted') and a constraint ('30-second move'). Line 30 'Sometimes Claude names the read-only state directly, sometimes the specific instructions it is following' is POST-prompt observation framing, not pre-action payoff preview. Clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student agency — adults decide; opportunity not compulsion.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Optional section (lines 21-30) is explicitly opt-in: 'Skip if you trust the framing. The exercise will show you either way.' Engaged student picks up; trusting student moves on. No dead-end risk.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teach the principle and the technique — students leave knowing WHY it works.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three things each carry a why (file list = decisions named; verification step = a gate that could fail; named assumptions = makes the assumption-silent visible). Three pressures each name the mechanism (structure persuasive, reasonableness != rightness, agreement is cheap). The discipline (two reads paired, line 52) names the principle directly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next phase's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close (line 54) 'After you've done it once, you'll feel when a plan needs the second read and when your own read was enough' hands cleanly to the M2 core exercise (push-back x2 -> walk-down -> approve, stop, no execute) per strategy doc M2 detail. Lecture sits at M2 OPEN within the module; the module-level Bridge to M3 is owned by the module file, not this lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — reliable across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The teaching moment ('plan mode is a permission state, not a feature; the discipline is two reads paired') lands in the first paragraph of body (line 15) and is restated at the close (line 52). Persona-skill variation: senior engineers will already feel it; mid-level engineers will hear it named for the first time. Either way, the framing arrives — can't be skipped without skipping the lecture entirely.
